description: Step-by-step beauty tutorials presented in bilingual (Arabic/English) captions with procedural instructions and product application techniques.
structural_recipe: Present a beauty tutorial as sequential steps: (1) Open with a relatable question or pain point in Arabic and English, (2) Show 3-5 clear procedural steps with close-up visuals, (3) Highlight key benefits (durability, precision, ease) in bilingual captions, (4) Use bullet points or numbered formatting, (5) Include brand handles or product mentions, (6) Close with aspirational outcome (glam, flawless look).
why_it_works: Saudi audiences respond to educational content that solves practical beauty challenges. Bilingual captions (Arabic-first) signal authenticity and accessibility. Step-by-step format reduces intimidation and positions products/creators as trusted guides. Weekend glam framing connects to social occasion culture.
transplantation_caveats: Requires bilingual execution—Arabic should lead, English follows
Tutorial length must be concise (15-30 sec) for retention
Product mentions should feel organic, not salesy, to avoid skepticism
Must demonstrate results on relatable skin tones for Saudi audience
cultural_constraints: Modest presentation of makeup application preferred over heavy glam messaging
Emphasis on 'lasting' and 'precise' appeals to preference for refined aesthetics
Weekend/occasion framing aligns with social event culture
